calendar.matters:
Motion To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Dec-09-2009 Off Calendar

Petition To Approve Settlement, Create Special Needs Trust, Modify Trusts, Direct Payment Of Attorney'S Fees And Court Costs To Petitioner'S Attorney, And Direct Payment Of Additional $5,000 Per Month For The Benefit Of Jennifer Kerner Not Subject To The Settlement Limitation
Off Calendar Dec-09-2009 Granted Per Written Order

Motion To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Nov-24-2009 Continued To Dec-09-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ian Sammis for Petitioner. Edward Thirkell for David Denebeim.

Mr. Thirkell stated that Ms. Saughter would not be appearing today, that the matter was resolved in mediation and that the settlement agreement required the drafting of a Special Needs Trust (SNT) for Jennifer. In addition, he stated that Ms. Saughter had two reservations about the SNT and that he could revise the stipulation to make it an order. Mr. Sammis argued that Ms. Saughter and her Client were not parties in the lawsuit, that the Trustee David Denebeim had total control of settling any disputes, that any additional delays were not in his Client's best interest, that the Court needed to take a look at it and that he did not want any further changes to the trust. The matter was continued to December 9, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court requested copies of all documents including the Special Needs Trust which are due by December 7, 2009. Counsel agreed to a $5,000.00 payment for one month to meet Jennifer's financial needs.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition To Approve Settlement, Create Special Needs Trust, Modify Trusts, Direct Payment Of Attorney'S Fees And Court Costs To Petitioner'S Attorney, And Direct Payment Of Additional $5,000 Per Month For The Benefit Of Jennifer Kerner Not Subject To The Settlement Limitation

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Apr-01-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Thirkell for David Denebeim (Trustee).

Mr. Thirkell informed the Court that the matter had settled and that he would be filing the Petition For Approval Of The Settlement and the Petition To Establish A Special Needs Trust. The matter was taken off calendar.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: T. Jackson.

Verified Petition To Remove Trustee For Damages For Negligent Infliction Of Emotional Distress, And Invasion Of Privacy
Off Calendar Feb-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Thirkell for and with David Dennebaum. Ian Sammis for the Beneficiary.

Mr. Thirkell provided status on Jennifer Kerner's evaluation and stated that mediation was scheduled to take place on March 23, 2009 before Betty Epstein. In addition, Mr. Thirkell requested a continuance in late April for status on mediation and then set the matter for trial, stated that discovery had not been conducted and stated that it would be a one week trial. The matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge, (206) to be set for trial, (order for the same was signed in open court). Status on mediation was set for April 1, 2009.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: R. Noguera/J. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Garrido.

Verified Petition To Remove Trustee For Damages For Negligent Infliction Of Emotional Distress, And Invasion Of Privacy
Off Calendar Dec-10-2008 Continued To Feb-04-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Verified Petition To Remove Trustee For Damages For Negligent Infliction Of Emotional Distress, And Invasion Of Privacy
Off Calendar Sep-16-2008 Advanced From Nov-19-08 To Oct-01-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Verified Petition To Remove Trustee For Damages For Negligent Infliction Of Emotional Distress, And Invasion Of Privacy
Off Calendar Oct-01-2008 Continued To Dec-10-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ian Michael Sammis for Jennifer Kerner. Ed Thirkell for David Denebeim (Trustee).

Mr. Thirkell stated that UCSF would do the evaluations, that Ms. Kerner was referred to the Kentfield Center for treatment, but he wasn't sure if she had started treatment. In addition, he stated Dr. McNeil would need her all of her medical records/information to properly evaluate her, that Walgreens would not release any information due to privacy issues, and noted that Ms. Kerner had waived her privacy rights by filing the lawsuit against his client. Mr. Sammis stated that he objected to David Denebeim having access to Ms. Kerner's records because he was worried he would not keep the information to himself. In addition, he stated that Ms. Kerner's therapy was to begin, and that the parties could start mediation while Ms. Kerner was in therapy. Mr. Thirkell stated that he was upset to hear that the therapy at Kentfield had not begun because his client had already sent them $4,400.00. In addition, he requested the date by which Ms. Kerner was to start the therapy. The Court stated that Ms. Kerner's treatment was to start the following week, continued the matter to December 10, 2008 for further status, and granted Mr. Thirkell's order releasing medical records (signing and order for the same in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR The Issue Regarding Payment Of Bills Incurred Prior To The Creation Of The Trust
Off Calendar Sep-19-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Thirkell for David Denebeim.

No appearance was made by Ian Sammis. Mr. Thirkell stated that he faxed a letter to Mr. Sammis, that he did not receive a response and requested the matter be denied. The matter was taken under submission. The November 19th status hearing date was advanced to October 1, 2008 at Mr. Thirkell's request.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Mann.

Verified Petition To Remove Trustee For Damages For Negligent Infliction Of Emotional Distress, And Invasion Of Privacy
Off Calendar Aug-06-2008 Continued To Nov-19-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ian Sammis for the Petitioner. Ed Thirkell for and with David Denebeim.

Mr. Sammis reported that the $4,800.00 bill had been paid, that they were still working on a method of getting the psychological evaluation done and that treatment, the evaluation and mediation should be completed by late October. The parties raised issues regarding Mr. Denebeim's responsibility to pay bills incurred prior to the creation of the trust, his responsibility to negotiate the reduction of medical bills and his responsibility for moving and finding placement for Ms. Kerner. The Court stated that it was the Trustee's duty to protect the trust and that part of that was to save the trust money. The Court also stated that it was ok to find a company that could deal with the medical bills. The matter was continued to November 19, 2008 for further status. The issue regarding payment of bills incurred prior to the creation of the trust is set for September 16, 2008. Each party is to submit their brief by August 27, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Leong.

Verified Petition To Remove Trustee For Damages For Negligent Infliction Of Emotional Distress, And Invasion Of Privacy
Off Calendar Jun-18-2008 Continued To Aug-06-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Thirkell for David Denebeim (Trustee). Ian Sammis for Jennifer Kerner (Petitioner).

Mr. Sammis stated that they were still negotiating as to who would do the psychological evaluation and requested a continuance. The matter was continued to August 6, 2008 for further status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith and A. Santiago.

Verified Petition To Remove Trustee For Damages For Negligent Infliction Of Emotional Distress, And Invasion Of Privacy
Off Calendar Mar-19-2008 Continued To Jun-18-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Thirkel for David Denebeim. Johanna Gomez (Specially appearing for Ian Sammis) for Jennifer Kerner (Petitioner).

Mr. Thirkel stated that the psychological and physical exams have been done. Ms. Gomez stated that the Doctor was having a hard time getting the referral to U.C. Mr. Thirkel stated that the only solution was to get an evaluation or to subpeona all records. Ms. Gomez will find out what the hold up is with the evaluation. The Court continued the matter to June 18, 2008 for status. If the status as to what has been done regarding the evalutaion is not definite, the Court will order a subpeona for all records.

Judicial Officer: Judge John Dearman; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: F. Smith; Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Verified Petition To Remove Trustee For Damages For Negligent Infliction Of Emotional Distress, And Invasion Of Privacy
Off Calendar Jan-16-2008 Continued To Mar-19-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Thirkell for Trustee. Ian Sammis for Petitioner.

Mr. Thirkell stated that the wording regarding the stipulation on the medical evaluation was being worked on. Mr. Samis stated that the medical evaluation had been postponed to ascertain the dollar amount of the medical expenses. The matter was continued to March 19, 2008 for further status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Verified Petition To Remove Trustee For Damages For Negligent Infliction Of Emotional Distress, And Invasion Of Privacy
Off Calendar Dec-19-2007 Continued To Jan-16-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Thirkell for David Denebeim (Trustee).

The Court questioned Mr. Sammis not being present. Mr. Thirkell reported that no medaition date had been set, that the wording on the stipulation had not been worked out , that UCSF needed referrals from Jennifer's medical doctors before doing the evaulations, and that the details on the transportation issue regarding Jennifer had not been worked out. Mr. Thirkell attempted to contact Mr. Sammis by phone but was unsuccessful. The matter was continued to January 16, 2008 for status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR #11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.

Verified Petition To Remove Trustee For Damages For Negligent Infliction Of Emotional Distress, And Invasion Of Privacy
Off Calendar Nov-07-2007 Continued To Dec-19-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ian Sammis for petitioner. Vincent Ogara and Ed Thirkel for and with David Denebeim (Trustee/Respondent).

Mr. Sammis expressed that the motion to strike opposition pleadings were in violation of the local rules, that they were not timely, and that there were $90,000.00 in unpaid medical bills. Mr. Thirkell requested that the motion to strike be denied, and stated that he sent a fax and mail to Mr. Sammis. In addition, Mr. Thirkel reported that his client had negotiated the medical bills reducing them to $28,000.00 and requested to have an independent medical exmination done for Ms. Kerner. The Court suggested mediation. Mr. Sammis expressed that mediation would not work if his client had to have face to face contact with Mr. Denebeim. Mr. Thirkell stated that mediation could be done without face to face contact and that his client was willing to pay for the examination. Mr. Thirkel will draft two stipulations, one regarding the independent medical examination at U. C. and one regarding mediation. The matter was continued to December 19, 2007 for a status report. The Court expects the stipulations to be signed, for Counsel to contact U.C. for the examination and the mediator . The status report should be filed at least 5 days before December 19, 2007. The Motion to Strike is denied.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: T. Bonner.
